Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. A Visual Introduction to Machine Learning (2015) 249 upvotes, 22 comments
  2. Learning athletic humanoid tennis skills from imperfect human motion data 24 upvotes, 0 comments
  3. Human Organ Atlas 63 upvotes, 4 comments
  4. Examples for the tcpdump and dig man pages 71 upvotes, 7 comments
  5. Zipp 2001 Restoration 14 upvotes, 0 comments
  6. SuperTux 0.7.0 21 upvotes, 1 comments
  7. C++26: The Oxford Variadic Comma 23 upvotes, 2 comments
  8. Rack-mount hydroponics 303 upvotes, 82 comments
  9. In Memoriam: John W. Addison, my PhD advisor 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  10. IBM, sonic delay lines, and the history of the 80×24 display (2019) 55 upvotes, 13 comments
  11. Show HN: Signet – Autonomous wildfire tracking from satellite and weather data 81 upvotes, 22 comments
  12. Kniterate Notes 37 upvotes, 8 comments
  13. Show HN: GDSL – 800 line kernel: Lisp subset in 500, C subset in 1300 27 upvotes, 5 comments
  14. A most elegant TCP hole punching algorithm 181 upvotes, 72 comments
  15. Generating All 32-Bit Primes (Part I) 58 upvotes, 18 comments
  16. Why Mathematica does not simplify sinh(arccosh(x)) 126 upvotes, 48 comments
  17. Separating the Wayland Compositor and Window Manager 67 upvotes, 23 comments
  18. Glassworm Is Back: A New Wave of Invisible Unicode Attacks Hits Repositories 110 upvotes, 43 comments
  19. Allow me to get to know you, mistakes and all 267 upvotes, 123 comments
  20. Grandparents are glued to their phones, families are worried [video] 71 upvotes, 33 comments
  21. Ask HN: How is AI-assisted coding going for you professionally? 21 upvotes, 7 comments
  22. The Webpage Has Instructions. The Agent Has Your Credentials 16 upvotes, 5 comments
  23. Show HN: What if your synthesizer was powered by APL (or a dumb K clone)? 47 upvotes, 24 comments
  24. UMD Scientists Create 'Smart Underwear' to Measure Human Flatulence 59 upvotes, 35 comments
  25. How kernel anti-cheats work 306 upvotes, 263 comments
  26. $96 3D-printed rocket that recalculates its mid-air trajectory using a $5 sensor 302 upvotes, 263 comments
  27. What makes Intel Optane stand out (2023) 100 upvotes, 80 comments
  28. The 100 hour gap between a vibecoded prototype and a working product 172 upvotes, 211 comments
  29. Treasure hunter freed from jail after refusing to turn over shipwreck gold 155 upvotes, 205 comments
  30. Hollywood Enters Oscars Weekend in Existential Crisis 60 upvotes, 148 comments

Last generated 2:30am, 16 March 2026. Source here.
